item_a.name: DoorDash
item_a.image_alt: DoorDash food delivery app interface showing restaurant listings and delivery options
pros: Largest restaurant selection with 450,000+ merchant partners
DashPass subscription offers $0 delivery fees on orders over $12
Fast average delivery times of 35-40 minutes
Superior driver availability in suburban and rural areas
cons: Higher service fees ranging from 11-15% per order
Delivery fees can reach $5-$8 without subscription
Customer support response times can be slow during peak hours
item_a.best_for: Users seeking the widest restaurant selection and fastest delivery times, especially outside major metropolitan areas.

item_b.name: Grubhub
item_b.image_alt: Grubhub food delivery app interface displaying menu options and order tracking features
pros: Grubhub+ membership includes free delivery and lower service fees
Better loyalty rewards program with frequent promotional offers
More transparent pricing with itemized fee breakdown
Strong presence in urban markets with exclusive restaurant partnerships
cons: Smaller merchant network with approximately 300,000 partners
Slower average delivery times of 45-50 minutes
Limited availability in rural and smaller suburban areas
item_b.best_for: Budget-conscious urban users who prioritize transparent pricing and loyalty rewards over selection variety.

comparison_summary: DoorDash dominates with 450,000+ restaurants and 35-40 minute deliveries, while Grubhub counters with clearer pricing and actually useful rewards. Unless you live in a major city and order constantly, DoorDash's speed and selection make it the better pick.
verdict_text: DoorDash wins for most people thanks to unbeatable restaurant selection and consistently faster deliveries, even if you'll pay slightly more per order.

key_differences.aspect: Minimum Order for Free Delivery (with subscription)

key_differences.item_b: Grubhub Rewards with points on every order, frequent bonus offers

seo_content:
I've tested both DoorDash and Grubhub extensively over the past year, and the choice between these food delivery giants really comes down to what matters most to you: breadth of options and speed, or transparent pricing and better rewards.

DoorDash has built the largest delivery empire in the country, partnering with over 450,000 merchants across 4,000+ cities in all 50 states. This massive network means you'll find pretty much any restaurant you're craving, especially if you live outside major cities where other services haven't expanded yet. I consistently see delivery times of 35-40 minutes with DoorDash, which beats the competition thanks to their huge driver network and smart routing technology. The DashPass subscription runs $9.99 monthly and eliminates delivery fees on orders over $12, which pays for itself fast if you order regularly.

Grubhub takes a different approach. With roughly 300,000 restaurant partners, the selection is smaller, but they've focused on quality over quantity with exclusive partnerships in urban areas. What I appreciate about Grubhub is the pricing clarity. Their service fees typically run 9-12% compared to DoorDash's 11-15%, making each order a bit cheaper. The Grubhub+ membership also costs $9.99 monthly with similar perks, but it stacks beautifully with their Grubhub Rewards program that gives you points on every purchase plus frequent bonus offers.

Delivery speed creates a real difference here. DoorDash averages 35-40 minutes while Grubhub takes 45-50 minutes. That extra 10 minutes might not sound like much, but when you're hungry or squeezing in lunch between meetings, it matters. On the flip side, Grubhub shows you an itemized breakdown of every fee, which addresses one of the biggest frustrations people have with delivery apps—hidden costs that inflate your total at checkout.

Where you live plays a big role in this decision. DoorDash's wider coverage makes it the only real option for suburban and rural users, while Grubhub concentrates on cities where they often have better restaurant variety. I've found Grubhub particularly strong in New York, Chicago, and San Francisco with local favorites you won't find on other platforms.

Without subscriptions, delivery fees look similar: $1.99-$8.99 on DoorDash and $0.99-$6.99 on Grubhub, though the actual cost depends on distance, demand, and which restaurant you're ordering from.

Both subscription plans cost the same $9.99 monthly, so the decision really hinges on your ordering patterns. If you value having every restaurant option available and need food delivered quickly, DoorDash wins. The selection is unmatched, especially for families or anyone not living in a dense downtown area. You can order from that specific Thai place you love without worrying whether the app supports it.

But if you're a city dweller who orders multiple times per week and wants to maximize value, Grubhub deserves a serious look. The lower service fees add up over time, and the rewards program actually feels rewarding rather than just marketing fluff. I've racked up enough points for free meals, which DoorDash doesn't really match.

For most people, I recommend DoorDash. The combination of selection, speed, and geographic reach makes it more practical day-to-day. You won't run into situations where your favorite restaurant isn't available, and getting food 10 minutes faster than the competition adds up to hours saved over a year. That said, if you live in a major city, order frequently, and like watching your dollars carefully, Grubhub's transparent pricing and meaningful rewards program could save you more money in the long run.

faq.q: Is DoorDash or Grubhub cheaper overall?
faq.a: Grubhub wins on individual order costs with lower service fees (9-12% versus 11-15%) and slightly cheaper delivery charges. But since both subscriptions cost the same $9.99 monthly, your total spend depends on how often you order and whether you actually use Grubhub's rewards program, which does offer better perks.

faq.q: Which service has faster delivery: DoorDash or Grubhub?
faq.a: DoorDash is consistently faster, averaging 35-40 minutes compared to Grubhub's 45-50 minutes. That 10-minute difference comes from having more drivers on the road and better routing algorithms. Times vary by location and restaurant, but DoorDash has the edge pretty much everywhere.

faq.q: Does DoorDash or Grubhub have more restaurants available?
faq.a: DoorDash crushes Grubhub here with 450,000+ restaurant partners versus 300,000+. DoorDash also covers way more areas geographically, making it your only real option in suburbs and rural areas. Grubhub focuses on cities where they sometimes have exclusive partnerships with popular local spots, but the overall selection isn't close.

faq.q: Are DashPass and Grubhub+ worth the subscription cost?
faq.a: At $9.99 monthly, either subscription pays for itself if you order 2-3 times per month since both eliminate delivery fees on orders over $12. Grubhub+ gives you better value if you combine it with their rewards points system. DashPass makes more sense if you want access to the biggest restaurant selection possible.

faq.q: Which delivery service treats restaurant partners and drivers better?
faq.a: Both charge restaurants 15-30% commission with terms varying by partnership tier. Driver pay is similar too, averaging $15-25 hourly including tips on both platforms. DoorDash drivers typically get more consistent work because of higher order volume, while Grubhub offers hourly guarantees in select markets, which provides more income stability.

Yes, DoorDash is better for most people thanks to its superior restaurant selection of 450,000+ locations and faster delivery times of 35-40 minutes. While Grubhub offers clearer pricing and better rewards, DoorDash's speed and variety make it the stronger choice unless you're heavily focused on maximizing cashback.

faq.q: Should I buy DoorDash or Grubhub?
faq.a: Choose DoorDash if you value speed, restaurant selection, and convenience—it has unbeatable availability in most areas. Pick Grubhub only if you order frequently and want transparent pricing with meaningful rewards, or if you live in a smaller market where DoorDash has limited options.

faq.q: What is the difference between DoorDash and Grubhub?
faq.a: DoorDash offers 450,000+ restaurants with faster 35-40 minute deliveries but charges slightly higher fees, while Grubhub provides clearer pricing and better rewards programs. DoorDash wins on selection and speed, making it ideal for most users, while Grubhub appeals to frequent, cost-conscious orders in major cities.

meta_title: DoorDash vs Grubhub (2026): Speed vs Savings Showdown
meta_description: Detailed comparison of DoorDash vs Grubhub. We break down restaurant selection, delivery speed, pricing, and geographic coverage to help you pick the right food delivery service.
comparison_intro: When deciding between DoorDash or Grubhub, it's easy to get caught up wondering which is better for your food delivery needs. The difference between DoorDash and Grubhub comes down to speed, selection, and price—factors that matter when you're hungry and want your meal fast. In our DoorDash vs Grubhub breakdown, we'll show you how DoorDash compared to Grubhub stacks up across restaurants, delivery times, and costs so you can pick the right service for your next order.
